plot_1.summary: Overall, the scatter plot is readable and communicates the intended relationship between energy above hull and band gap, but several small issues reduce clarity.
issues: Some markers overlap heavily near zero on the x-axis, making individual points hard to distinguish.
Tick labels on the x-axis extend to 0.05 eV/atom but most data are clustered below 0.02; scaling could be adjusted for better resolution of dense region.
No grid lines are provided, which could aid in reading approximate values.
Point transparency (alpha) is not used, which would help in regions of over-plotting.
improvements: Consider using a logarithmic or slightly zoomed x-axis (e.g., limit to 0–0.02 eV/atom or break the axis) to emphasize the clustered data while still indicating outliers.
Add slight transparency and/or smaller marker size or jitter to reduce overlap.
Introduce subtle grid lines to enhance readability without clutter.
Optionally annotate median lines or density shading to convey distribution along both axes.

plot_7.summary: Overall a clear scatter plot with sensible title, axis labels, and units. Data range is appropriate and axes scales appear linear and reasonable.
issues: Dense overlap of points around 0 eV/atom makes it hard to see individual data—no transparency or jitter applied.
No gridlines; judging exact values is slightly harder.
Marker size is moderately large, contributing to overplotting.
Tight layout could be used to reduce surrounding whitespace.
improvements: Add slight alpha transparency or reduce marker size to improve visibility in dense regions.
Enable gridlines or minor ticks for easier reading of approximate values.
Apply plt.tight_layout() (or equivalent) to optimise whitespace.

plot_3.summary: The scatter conveys the negative correlation clearly and both axes are labeled with appropriate units. Title is descriptive and the overall layout is uncluttered.
issues: Marker overlap at low-energy region hides point density, reducing readability.
No grid lines or reference lines; makes it harder to judge approximate values.
Font size on axis labels and ticks is a bit small relative to figure size.
Energy-axis starts at 0 but density axis does not; visual balance feels off.
improvements: Add slight transparency (alpha) or smaller markers to reveal dense clusters.
Enable a subtle grid or horizontal reference line at E=0 to aid value estimation.
Increase label and tick font sizes for better legibility in presentations.
Consider setting the same style for both axes baselines or add padding for symmetry.

plot_4.summary: Clear, straightforward histogram with appropriate axis labels and units; data distribution is easy to read.
issues: Tick-label font size could be larger for better readability, especially on presentations.
Consider adding subtle gridlines to aid visual estimation of frequencies.
The figure margins are a bit tight on the left and bottom, slightly crowding labels.
improvements: Increase tick-label and axis-label font sizes or change figure DPI for enhanced legibility.
Add light gridlines to improve value tracking across the plot.
Provide a bit more padding around the plot area to avoid label crowding.

plot_5.summary: Plot communicates overall shape of density distribution clearly, but several presentation aspects reduce professionalism and ease of interpretation.
issues: Y-axis lacks a descriptive title such as "Count" or "Frequency" (currently only "Frequency").
Tick labels are small; font size mismatch between title, axes, and ticks hampers readability.
Bin count appears high, leading to many sparse bars on the right tail; this makes the histogram look noisy and breaks visual focus.
Plot frame is quite empty because of extreme right-side outliers; autoscaling leaves large white region in the centre.
No information about sample size in caption or plot.
Figure uses default style; colours and gridlines could help interpret density clusters.
improvements: Rename y-axis to a clearer term (e.g., "Count of samples").
Increase tick-label font size to match axis titles for better legibility.
Consider reducing number of bins or using adaptive binning to smooth frequencies, especially given long tail.
Apply x-axis limits or break to reduce empty space or plot two panels (main and zoomed-in) to handle outliers.
Add gridlines or subtle background cues to assist reading frequencies.
If relevant, note sample size in subtitle or caption.
Optionally use a contrasting colour palette or edge colour to distinguish bars.

issues: Y-axis lacks a descriptive title such as "Count" or "Frequency" (currently only "Frequency").
Tick labels are small; font size mismatch between title, axes, and ticks hampers readability.
Bin count appears high, leading to many sparse bars on the right tail; this makes the histogram look noisy and breaks visual focus.
